en.extract:
The Chin Hills wren-babbler (Spelaeornis oatesi) is a bird species in the family Timaliidae. It was until recently considered a subspecies of the long-tailed wren-babbler; the IUCN for example started recognizing it as distinct species in 2008.
It is found in India and Myanmar. Its natural habitat are subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It is therefore classified as a Species of Least Concern by the IUCN.


Footnotes


References
BirdLife International (BLI) (2008): 2008 IUCN Redlist status changes. Retrieved 2008-MAY-23.

The '''Chin Hills wren-babbler''' ('''''Spelaeornis oatesi''''') is a bird species in the [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Timaliidae]]. It was until recently considered a [[subspecies]] of the [[long-tailed wren-babbler]]; the [[IUCN]] for example started recognizing it as distinct species in 2008.<ref name = bli2008>BLI (2008)</ref>

It is found in [[India]] and [[Myanmar]]. Its natural habitat are subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. It is therefore classified as a Species of [[Least Concern]] by the IUCN.<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" /><ref name = bli2008 />
